subject: Cloud Computing
title: In-Memory Caching with Redis
description: One-minute skill: In-Memory Caching with Redis.

hook.text: A database query takes 50 milliseconds. The same data from Redis takes 0.5 milliseconds. That's 100x faster.

buildup.text: Redis stores data in RAM instead of on disk. Memory is thousands of times faster than solid-state drives.

discovery.text: Redis handles session data, leaderboards, rate limiting, and real-time analytics — anything that needs speed.

twist.text: RAM is expensive and limited. You can't cache everything. Choosing what to cache — and when to evict — is the art.

climax.text: ElastiCache, MemoryDB, and Azure Cache for Redis make it managed. You get the speed without the operational overhead.

punchline.text: Redis is fast because RAM is fast. Cache wisely.

quiz.question: Why is Redis so much faster than traditional databases?
options: It stores data in RAM, which is thousands of times faster than disk
It uses a special compression algorithm that speeds up queries
It only supports simple data types that are faster to process
